[=Theorem \ref{thm main theorem for compact}]  
	\label{thm compact in introduction}  
	Let \((W, \phi: W \to W)\) satisfy the hypotheses in Lemma \ref{lem compact entropy}.  
	Let \(\Phi_{\mathcal{F}(W)}\) denote the functor induced by \(\phi\) on the compact Fukaya category \(\mathcal{F}(W)\).  
	Then the categorical entropy \(h_{cat}(\Phi_{\mathcal{F}(W)})\) bounds the topological entropy of \(\phi\) from below:  
	\[h_{cat}(\Phi_{\mathcal{F}(W)}) \leq h_{top}(\phi).\]

context_pack.exact_source_excerpt: \begin{lem} \label{lemma Lagrangian tomograph} Let $(L_1, L_2)$ be a good pair of Lagrangians in $\hat{W}$. Then, for any $\epsilon >0$ and sufficiently large $d \in \mathbb{N}$, there is a sufficiently small $\delta>0$ and a family of Lagrangians $\{L^s\}_{s \in B_\epsilon^d}$, where $B_\epsilon^d$ is a $d$-dimensional closed ball of radius $\delta$ in the Euclidean space, such that \begin{enumerate} \item[(i)] $L_1$ and $L^s$ are Hamiltonian isotopic to each other for all $s \in B_\epsilon^d$, \item[(ii)] $d_H(L_1, L^s) < \frac{\epsilon}{2}$ for all $s \in B^d_\epsilon$, and \item[(iii)] $L^s$ and $L_2$ intersect transversely for almost all $s \in B_\epsilon^d$. \end{enumerate} \end{lem}
